intro: Baeothryon retroflexum, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai, is a perennial plant native to China that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, offering various health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps and improving blood circulation. Its bioactive constituents include volatile oils, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
